skill.name: scholar-evaluation
skill.description: Implements the ScholarEval framework to evaluate scholarly documents; trigger when the user provides a PDF/DOCX/TXT file or pasted text and requests critique, scoring, or quality assessment.
skill.summary: Evaluate scholarly documents using an 8-dimension rubric with weighted scoring and revision recommendations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 31 static analysis findings are false positives. The 6 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched non-cryptographic terms (score, weight) in rubric documentation, JSON audit data, and argparse code. The 5 'system reconnaissance' detections matched rubric text and JSON field names. The 17 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections flag markdown code formatting (inline code and fenced bash blocks) in SKILL.md, not actual executable commands. The 2 'hardcoded URL' findings reference a legitimate GitHub source repository. This skill is a legitimate academic evaluation framework using Python scripts for document processing with no malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution in Documentation (False Positive)
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 17 instances of shell backtick syntax in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell execution. All instances are markdown code formatting: inline code references to script names (e.g., scripts/extract_text.py) and fenced code blocks showing bash usage examples. These are documentation, not executable code. The skill does instruct running Python scripts for file extraction and score calculation, which is legitimate academic tooling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at 6 locations. The flagged files (evaluation_framework.md, scholar-evaluation_audit_result_v1.json, calculate_scores.py, SKILL.md) contain no cryptographic code. Flagged content includes rubric text about 'weight', JSON scoring metadata, argparse setup, and skill description text. None of these implement or reference cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'System reconnaissance' at 5 locations. Flagged content includes rubric evaluation text about citation styles, JSON data field names in the audit result, and an error message string. No system reconnaissance, directory traversal, or information gathering behavior exists in this skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation (False Positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub URL at SKILL.md:7 as a hardcoded network address. This URL is the legitimate source repository link for the skill (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills), documented as a reference in the SKILL.md header. It is not used for data exfiltration or unauthorized network communication.

content.user_title: Evaluate and Score Academic Papers with ScholarEval
content.value_statement: Academic researchers struggle to evaluate scholarly work with consistent criteria. This skill applies a structured 8-dimension rubric to score and critique research papers, theses, and proposals.
actual_capabilities: Extract text from PDF, DOCX, and TXT files using Python scripts
Score scholarly documents across 8 evaluation dimensions with 1 to 5 scale
Calculate weighted aggregate scores with configurable dimension weights
Generate structured critique reports with strengths, weaknesses, and recommendations
Compare multiple document versions using consistent rubric-based scoring
Assess submission readiness for conferences and journals
limitations: Text extraction requires Python dependencies (pypdf, python-docx) installed locally
Scoring follows a fixed rubric and does not adapt to field-specific criteria
Cannot evaluate figures, tables, or visual content within documents
Results depend on the quality of text extracted from source files

use_cases.title: Pre-Submission Quality Check
use_cases.description: Evaluate a research paper against academic writing standards before journal submission to identify weaknesses and prioritize revisions.

use_cases.title: Thesis Draft Review
use_cases.description: Score each chapter of a thesis or dissertation using consistent criteria to track improvement across multiple revision cycles.

use_cases.title: Grant Proposal Assessment
use_cases.description: Assess the clarity and rigor of research proposals to determine which submissions to develop further or prioritize.
use_cases.target_user: Research office staff and grant reviewers

prompt_templates.title: Basic Paper Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this research paper using the ScholarEval rubric. Provide scores for each dimension with brief justification.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick assessment of a single paper

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Critique with Recommendations
prompt_templates.prompt: Critique the attached manuscript across all 8 ScholarEval dimensions. For each dimension, assign a score from 1 to 5, explain your reasoning, and list specific revision recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Comparative Draft Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two versions of the same manuscript using the ScholarEval framework. Highlight which dimensions improved, which regressed, and summarize the overall score difference.

prompt_templates.title: Submission Readiness Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this paper for submission readiness. Calculate the overall weighted score and identify the top 3 critical weaknesses that must be addressed before submission. Flag any dimension scored below 3.

output_examples.input: Research paper PDF on machine learning for medical diagnosis
output_examples.output: Dimension scores with per-criteria justifications, an overall weighted score of 3.85 out of 5, and a prioritized list of 5 recommended revisions targeting the lowest-scoring dimensions.

output_examples.input: Two draft versions of a literature review chapter
output_examples.output: A side-by-side comparison showing improvement in 5 dimensions, regression in 1 dimension, and an overall score increase from 3.2 to 3.8 with specific notes on changed sections.

output_examples.input: Grant proposal on climate change impact assessment
output_examples.output: Structured feedback highlighting strong problem formulation at score 4, weak methodology section at score 2 requiring major revision, and 3 specific recommendations to strengthen the proposal.

best_practices: Run text extraction before evaluation for PDF and DOCX files to ensure clean text input for the rubric scoring
Review each dimension score justification to verify rubric criteria are correctly applied to the document content
Use the comparative analysis feature when tracking revisions across multiple manuscript versions
anti_patterns: Do not skip text extraction for PDF files and expect the AI to read visual content or embedded images directly
Do not modify dimension scores without adjusting the corresponding qualitative justification to match
Do not use this skill for non-academic writing such as marketing copy, technical documentation, or creative writing

faq.question: What file formats does the text extraction support?
faq.answer: PDF, DOCX, and TXT files. The extraction uses pypdf for PDF, python-docx for DOCX, and standard text reading for TXT files.

faq.question: How are the dimension weights configured?
faq.answer: Weights are defined in calculate_scores.py. Problem Formulation, Methodology, and Analysis have the highest weights at 15 to 20 percent each.

faq.question: Can I evaluate text without providing a file?
faq.answer: Yes. You can paste text directly and request evaluation without file extraction. The same 8-dimension rubric applies.

faq.question: What is the scoring scale?
faq.answer: Each dimension is scored from 1 (Poor) to 5 (Excellent). The overall weighted score combines all dimensions proportionally.

faq.question: Does this skill work with scanned PDF documents?
faq.answer: No. Scanned PDFs contain images of text, not extractable text. Use OCR software before submitting scanned documents.

faq.question: How do I install the required Python dependencies?
faq.answer: Run pip install -r requirements.txt to install pypdf, PyPDF2, and python-docx libraries.
